she will not be your soft companion

grants you sly acknowledgments

she will not understand or care to,
who makes the rain beat furiously down on houses
then shows her humid teeth,
which are brilliant at noon

she will not be collegial in her assessments of you
or measured in her actions.
this is not what you had hoped for in your adolescence

such timid intimations, you
pusillanimous whelp 
vacillating f*ggot

demand it! do not suggest. 
make lines of
demarcation. 

and if you want a soft companion
you will not find her in the garden
or in the whole wide world,
and how wide it is
how full and teasing in its curvature

who was Eurydice again?
some Greek broad

I looked her up on Wikipedia 
and skimmed the article

I didn't find anything in that article
that I was interested in knowing

I didn't find anything in the links
at the bottom of the page

that would indicate to me
why the name "Eurydice" would come to me

in a midnight desperation
free write

having watched some movie
that made me feel like I was in the movie

and could be something of meaning
to myself and others

and that the world is not an ersatz Pleasure Dome
in fucky n-th dimension Hellscapes

where smiles are experimental
and pain is food
that vanishes on the threshold of the mouth

      in this dim Planetarium
      where the milky dust swirls
      in blue projected beams

      who are restrain-ed there below
            to wish upon one sweetly
             for it seems 
            that they might catch it falling, oh
      I would fain greet thee

	in storied configurations

Text:
you can hear my words, and I can hear yours. we can grasp at the notions, dimly.
but you will never feel the world quite as I do; there is a gap.
only blood can truly know that--only those who occupy a shared line,
with a head the shape of mine, and an eye of a similar hue.

To Chuck
if you are still alive
in some wallpapered room
intubated or lying unturned,
I beg of you to
speak a word or two
through these dim passages
and ease the frayed connection

what did you give my mother
to give to me

where did you wrongly go
at the point of veering

and what do you know?
can you solve a difficult puzzle
in less than the average time?
have you committed whole works to memory?

and are you large in your old age,
ungainly and unsure of foot?

do you feel the weight of all this,
of the many errors,
the horrid glimpses of possibility?

maybe you have a little book of poems
and that's enough for you,
as it is for me.

maybe a fleshless hand winds the
dark-jeweled mechanism
to mark the turning
of your cold notches into mine

Text: I am a person-shaped mirror. I need a real person, a solid object to give me a body and eyes

the 10 billion names of God
are recorded in a manual
with diagrams of instruments

unassembleable,
these small machines are oddly-
numbered and exploded

the fittings pass electrically
and are corroded

Build! the stuttering baggage carts
and a second-headed woman
singing Chinese harmonies
on a whiteish slab. Build!

I think there to be 
a light in the oldest buildings
that hovers and grows
from strands of energy 

that people shed in passing through or living there
it drifts like hair
to clump the spirit drain

what it produces 
is a source of power

the orbs that Galileo dropped from 
his leaning tower
were two of them
two of the names that he knew
of the many that he knew
(Jupiter, Saturn)
and he bore one himself

***

the fat baby
shakes fortuna's
rattle
flaking lead

it's in the drinking water of 
ne'er-do wells who
think to make God
by piecen piece
who take the plans and follow them,
concatechant

and the fat baby gurgles in the dark old buildings
and the sound brings dragons 
in gonging spasmody

and the sound brings women dancing
and the promise of warmth 
and a name to say

flying machines once went like pterodactyls overhead
and roosted on the tenements
the patent office was a rattling cave
from coconut trams

Adam patented Eve;
Cain knew 5 billion names;
I think there is a spiritual appendage,
like Samson
I think there is a gathering place,
like the Alleghany
it has a cold mouth to say it:
Build! Build! Build!

Tiberius looked up squinting in the low sun. Sitting on a trailside log, he chewed his sandwich, then addressed The Moppy Headed Man beside him. "knock it off."

"what."

"with the glibness."

"me? I'm glib, yeah. Okay. you're the glib one."

"see," Tiberius swallowed, "and that's it right there."

The Moppy Headed Man thumbed his nose and yawned.

"See, again. you can't help it. everything you do is so acted out and-" he minced, "per-fect-ly askew."

TMHM slackened his face retardedly, tongue protruding.

"it's like a lace doily, placed ironically. Placed too consciously. Somewhat." He struggled. "It's like a lace doily on a minifridge"

"Okay," TMHM laughed, "first of all, you don't know what glib means. You also don't know what shrill means, you misuse that one all the time too. You use words in a vague way and think it's cool somehow. Also-"

Tiberius tossed his crust, "Also?"

"A doily? If you can't, you know, pull the right image, settle for a cliche. You don't have to be inventive at all times."

"Buddy, if I were trying to be inventive, you'd know. Glib is, a kind of slickness, or- or coyness.."

"It's neither of those things."

"It's both of them and it's what you are. Finish the sandwich-- and stop showing me your fucking mouth like that."

TMF hawked his bolus and displayed. They got up from the log and continued down the path, which was publicly maintained and cut through a tract of state-owned pine and scrub.

When the two had done their weekly loop, they smoked at the cars, and drove off to their homes on opposite sides of town, where each would look at their phones until sleep came, if it came, and sometimes it did not, which was the case tonight for Tiberius, wincing at blunders, missed cues, and unsaid rejoinders that his younger self would have seized upon. That was getting older, anyhow, missing more and more of it, slowing down and gearing up for the long interminable stretches. It was conservation, he thought dubiously, storing up soul; and it was additive too, wisdom gained in greater measure than faculties lost. This was what one would be judged on in the last moments--the accumulated weight of meaningful silences. Words, if anything, were deductions from that total, and evinced attachments to category which--Tiberius gestured in the darkness--held one back on the final day. Yes, that was it; these failings of mind were instruments of grace, a sacramental beckoning-toward-void. The temple must needs be desecrated that a new and loftier one might rise; this was no tragedy, or it was, but in the highest sense--like Christ and Him crucified. And every slip or lost foothold along that road to Calvary, every lash of senility's whip, was the purgation of a piece of one's consciousness, which was the original sin--to Know--whose hissing enticements had turned us to snakes. Only once the scales of knowledge had been sloughed off, naked of mind and raw of spirit, could we rise to hind legs once more, with arms outstretched in grinning cruciform, and lumber back into the primaeval garden that was our just ape's inheritance. And- and that was how it was.

Satisfied with that, Tiberius rolled over. "Glib," he muttered serenely.

Text:
this is what I can manage
and the best I can do
sifting refuse on the dais
til the old cow lows

barely connected, 
my thoughts do not assign themselves properly;
faces on screens appear strange
and distances grow 
weirdly on a fourth axis

or a fifth one
that some severe Father
might call insight

collapse, collapse
fall in
return and become sensible

but this is what I can manage
from my high remove
restrained like Tityos
atop my heap

dumbly entreating 
the carrion birds to shoo, shoo
to fetch me smokes
the size of cedar trees

only those will do me
a phat Phokian dart
to clear my giant head

oh

who will milk her swollen udders,
who will relieve them,
the leaking teats of the lowing cow
that grazes,
save me?

[derivative, feckless, and lacking in
merit,
who will number among footnotes
least among them
as a minor mythic figure
with arms that could not reach?]

Text: the "center" is a position defined by extremes. shift the extremes and you shift the center. (see the Overton Window). there is no neutrality.

solarpunk is an emerging ecofuturist movement that seeks to integrate human technologies with the natural world, based on principles of ecological sustainability, cross cultural awareness, and communal practice. Its utopian vision of a "Symbiocene" wherein civilizational infrastructures are congruent with, dependent upon, and consciously consititutive of the organic systems that gave rise to them may seem starry-eyed, but proponents point to the many hidden technologies that indigenous populations have used for millenia to supply necessities through mutually productive (rather than merely extractive) means--to wit, irrigation techniques, living bridges, and other examples of biotechnics that are replicable, repairable, and sustainable without reliance upon industrial mechanisms. this would, of course demand a paradigmatic shift toward small-scale, long-term planningz rather than the massive myopic emdeavors that have defined the post-industrial anthropocene. it's a compelling vision, but its feasibility in a world that is precariously and radically reliant upon the extraction of hard-to-come-by resources is dubious, absent some precipitating ELE that demands/necessitates a reorientation of such magnitude. for now, its the realm of sci fi writers, the self-proclaimed ecotects, and hopelessly optimistic theorists who dare to dream.

simply put: solarpunk is an aesthetic, ecological, and communitarian movement that imagines a future wherein our collective cosmotechnics are Earth-aligned such that human life functions toward the betterment of, and not at the expense of, other forms of life, a goal best acheived by symbiotic (re)design.

Text:
some people say that ssris blunt affect and limit one's emotional range, that they deprive one of the means to spiritual growth by suppressing inner turmoil. but recently, I saw a post on a forum calling them "enlightenment drugs," saying that they cleared the path to sublimity, for certain kinds of people.

I think that's true and I think that I must be that kind of person, because I did nearly all of my best writing and thinking on these drugs, and I grew spiritually by leaps and bounds. 

I've been off them for several months now, and since then have reverted to a decidedly worse version of myself: irascible, dull, morose, unfocused, distant. 

it's been a special kind of hell, one that I've continued to endure for two reasons: 1. sex (embarrassingly and very unenlightened-ly), and 2. a deep desire to shed dependencies of all kinds.

i tell myself, well, I've lost the things that I treasured most about myself, but they were never mine to begin with it. it's good that I have lost them; it's good for my ego to suffer this deprivation; I will adapt, I will do what I can with the bare stuff nature has afforded me. 

and there's something to that maybe. with counseling, with lifestyle changes, with tremendous and prolonged effort, I could possibly rebuild a stable creative core-- backward engineer my way to the tranquility, clarity, and playfulness of my medicated self-- something comparable to it, or maybe even better. 

maybe maybe. but likely not. life is short; mental faculties fade; my time is limited. 
if meds grant me a shortcut to self-potentiation/-realization, if they help me access and activate my fast-waning capacities, isn't it foolish to spurn them for the sake of false dignity or existential parsimony?

isn't it my sacred duty, to the extent I have a duty at all, to wring myself dry--to make everything I can make by whatever means before insensate darkness reclaims my deathbound matter?

I do not know. what I know is this: there's fuel left in the tank. but the ignition system is compromised, and the electrical reservoirs are drained. automatic means of reinvigoration are readily available, but I stubbornly, futilely insist on hand-cranking. 

maybe I will sit here then, a little roadside monument to waste and stupidity, and gather rust. maybe that is the truest way.

the creative process is curational and museal. we do not produce or generate the artifacts, but rather inherit them (often questionably), preserve them (often jealously), and recontextualize them (often misleadingly) for display purposes. dark markets keep this whole tenuous circuit open, by way of counterfeiting or downright theft. valuation rises, but only so long as the tacitness of the sordid business is maintained.

(thanks to K.)

good advice or teaching does not necessarily divulge what is hidden; it hides or sets aside what is merely visible so that the invisible factors at play, long suppressed, can rise to conscious knowledge.
it is a process of removing blockages, not of filling gaps.
categorical value judgments are the recourse of the simple minded or the utterly propagandized.

the malice/derangement of the arsonist does not lessen or profane the power, clarity, and potency of the flame. rather, it attests to it. the greater a system's rapacity for evil, the greater its capacity for good.

this is what makes the portrait so compelling--it is a broken mirror, turned off.

metal is cartographic, archaeological; it maps out and excavates discrete, cordoned off zones of dark interior (whether chthonic or cosmic or tundral as the sub-sub-genre would have it).
it _takes place_ there over long periods; it feeds there and mutates; it is _situated_, anthropologically and aesthetically and chronologically. there is a scientific precision to its advance, its occult devotion to the Old Names. each passage and chamber is liturgized, each stygian encounter has precedent; death is a matter of record here and fear is technical, an arcane annotation.

symbol is not merely received but enacted and enforced: referent > reference > referee.

we preside in the latter capacity (as referee) over the field of indifferent amoral imageplay, flagging the interactional/infractional instance, imparting to it the weight of meaning and the order of rule.

it is the presence of man-as-official and the act of symbolic officiation that renders life "gameful", that grants it the competitive arc, that enables us to "keep score." 

and so, symbol is executive and judiciary; it is not legislative. critical as it is to the notion of "fair play"-- artistically, societally, and in every other way-- symbolic hygiene must be maintained, not by the referee but by some other higher body (the "Board") who prunes, appends, and manages the book of signs.

let us say the referee is artisan and maker; let us say the board is priest and king.

Text:
art does not convey or contain a message; it is one.
you cannot extract the thing from itself, only subtract or detract. 
so the gourmand, literary cutlery in hand, rends the fragile structure of the whole, finds no satiety in his frowning bites, and with every probing cut ensures his everlasting hunger.

fetish runs counter to utility. the expedient trait or object-- the laborer's musculature, or the penitent's whip-- are embedded in causal relations toward an end and are, thus, invisible.
fetish develops in isolation from and excess of this invisible set of utilities. 
it is not the woman's foot that the fetishist worships, but the perverse superfluity of its mechanics as regards the erotic; comorbid with commodity, it is a thing-shaped absence in the active world-set, a waste-product--the form without function of a relic behind glass. museal carnality possesses us; conveying nothing, we are conveyed by belts from wonder to wonder. for viewing pleasure only, do not touch, and semen lubricates the assembly line of desire.

"Human enterprise has two aspects: it is both success and failure...Poetry is a case of the loser winning. And the genuine poet chooses to lose, even if he has to go so far as to die, in order to win...Thus, if one absolutely wishes to speak of the engagement of the poet, let us say that he is the man who engages himself to lose....it is his deepest choice, the source, and not the consequence of his poetry. He is certain of the total defeat of the human enterprise and arranges to fail in his own life in order to bear witness, by his individual defeat, to human defeat in general. Thus, he contests, as we shall see, which is what the prose-writer does too. But the contesting of prose is carried one in the name of a greater success; and that of poetry, in the name of the hidden defeat which every victory conceals."

at once and for all.

Text:
I used to think that emotions were cancerous, that they took hold of you and ravaged you. they were, I believed, things to be excised or irradiated--malignant growths.

Now, I begin to see them differently--as indicators--not as a source of harm but as symptomatic of underlying conditions or states that must be heeded, savored; only then do they subside.

"in twenty minutes' time we shall
or at such point we must"
Satre was correct, I see, 
to say that poets represent
and do not signify,
at least not straightaway or by design.

the word is object, or so it becomes
when gazed upon as mirrors are
to occupy a state,
transformed into a body that is not a body--

the poet cannot choose
he can only permit or be permitted 
by his strange carapace
more oddly shaped than most,
formed of a great many scales and pieces, 
each a set of words
that volunteer themselves 
to some expressive end.

so does the poet end up naked,
having used his shell to make these bodies that are not
for others to put on at will
and feel language the other way round,
a sock turned inside out.

sarte was correct, I see.
this is not existence,
and it is not the poet's to exist.
for he shies from the anguish
of word's malign utility.

he is animal,
and a frail one,
who consorts with other animals--
the musician and the painter--
these beasts of temperament, 
becoming naked.

not so the great Prosodist,
who Exists in surfeit,
cloth-ed utterly and attended by pomp,
a tyrant and king, 
a sly knave,
an engineer of lofty works,
a layer of plans.
It is he, extant, who
engages the word with purpose and aplomb
to End and to Begin a thing,
though to uncertain effect,
which certitude grants furtherance
in many words to come.

Text: expression is ontological leakage; impression is epistemological contamination.

i dont feel represented, in any meaningful way, by the things i do or say. which is why when i produce something "good" it amazes me, and why i have no real sexual scruple, why i am able to discuss deeply personal matters dispassionately. 

it seems that I perform myself through a discontinuous series of word- and act-instances that--on the _felt_ level-- have no bearing upon or connection to the wildly fluctuating state of H.-ness and the inarticulable experience of that state.

intellectually, though, i recognize and know that others presume such continuities to exist--though they don't and can't-- between what it is to be H. and what it is to act as H. they (others) assign to each instance a permanent weight, not merely as perceptual but as actual.

this gives me pause and concerns me greatly. it also excites, as it offers some approximate measure of self, received vicariously through the eye of the other. my hesitation at the threshhold of exchange, which is to say being fixedly known, outweighs, or else subsumes, the desire to thereby fixedly know, and so the gap widens, swallowing expression; no transfer can occur, if any ever did. i become a void to others, and so--incidentally, trivially, as it so happens--a void is what I must be.

the cat in the window

while isotopic snow fell roundabout

on hoods of cars

gone
that break in the fence
made 50 years ago or more
churchgoers in the appleyard
gone gone

the white cuts on the sky
that aircraft leave
have scarred to distant blue

and I have lost my song

Text:
the yellow car 
of Hecate
may not be hailed 
at any dreary corner

its plush interior
yields to but one or two
a night

the thousand eyed foreigner,
say, 
Xanadu bound
whose dogs are tethered
loosely
to a streetlight

which grows long 
filaments
and bats them 
at the sailors passing

lucky man,
to be the object 
of such airs
as blow sweetly from that
slow mouth

to send out ships 
on odyssey
their tall pine masts
jutting 
though the fabric
of a low-slung sky

such airs 
and what a lucky one
to die, secretly,
upon a torrid errand

A., dearest,
foolish one,
best one,
I cannot kiss that thing upon the slab
I must find leaves to clothe you
or else to use for salve (the wound is grevious, oh the wound is shame)

Opened once, it closes fast again,
and who will pick the lock?

work is the task you are set to by some figure, a parent or parent proxy; Work is the Task you set yourself to. the former is childish, necessary, (incentivized by necessity), and able-to-be-done; the latter, as a state of actualized alignment between potential and the kinetic, is indefinite, superfluous, heroic, and maturational--the mark of the Man.
happy is he whose work is his Work.
happier still is he whose Work is his all.

Text:
Remembering that THIS is life. I am in it at this moment and this moment and this moment. 
Even when "present," I find I am simply imagining a future wherein I am "present," or imagining myself imagining that.
It does not "get better." There is no then-state/later-state to bring about the long-awaited.
Only now-state and now-state and now-state. This is life, I'm in it now.
Remembering that. Thanks.

Text:
i have no moral obligation to softness. the world is hard, as are people and words. they have edges. to speak is to be is to risk cutting and being cut.

a sense of woundedness on your part does not constitute wrongdoing on mine

i went to a witch named edna on an internet forum

understand
this is not a poem.

she claims to offer insight from beyond
through astral means

when I stage my plaint to her, falteringly,
there is some some sense
of travel and receipt
like wing-ed Mercury 
missal borne

though this is not a poem
   it lacks the characteristic marks
still it feels like movement
and still it feels like handled clay

so too the rendering of Edna's 
correspondence
   some higher principle at work
die struck to form and cast 
among the stars
which relay my signallings to her
and hers to mine
 in a faint but wondrous way

Text: the terror and trepidation i feel at the thought of having to earn an ego, to live toward Greatness (before I can die to it) probably means I'm onto something, or have been put onto it.

anything worth saying can be said in a sentence.
but sometimes it takes a tome to arrive at that sentence.

"the delusive screen of individuality"

"technologies" of ego are projective, medial, illusory, several degrees removed from what is real. 
i've always assumed this to be true.

but what if ego is not a false, intervening surface, but rather the "real thing" that the whole apparatus of negation and paradox and moral scruple was built to suppress.

in other words, what if I'm not imprisoned _in_ the cave; I'm imprisoned by the Cave, by the Analogy that positions me as bound, inert, and in need of ascent and "absorption" into Something Greater.

what if it's not transcendence i require but reindividuation.

what if Will is the breath of God in me, and attempts to deny or surrender that breath are a suicidal revocation.

I'm revisiting Nietzsche, as is probably obvious. I'm not a stranger to his ideas, but for some reason, I've never really felt their weight. 

Imagining, even experimentally, that the Will might be holy, something to be exercised fully and volitionally (not just penitentially relinquished), fills me a sense of urgency and potency I've never quite felt. maybe that's just Mania talking and not real Joy.

I'm still an ascetic-mystic in my bones, but for the first time i'm questioning whether that's...good

and it's embarrassing to admit--feels very juvenile, Philosophy 101 type shit--but I have really never considered what religion is doing on a mechanical fundamental level as regards the self.
anxiety is ballast, depression too. without them, you sail like icarus toward the sun. in excess, you never leave the ground

compassion and honesty do not comport.
if you want a woman's warmth and sympathy, you must endure and accept her mendacity.
this leads us to the very horrible fact that there is no true kindness. it is a social act; the kinder the person the more skilled at dissimulating, at making you (and themselves) believe that they feel things they do not.

an echo chamber is a place where your own opinions get endlessly repeated back to you, generating a shared sense of fanatical (false) certainty via confirmation bias. The community is self policing and hostile to outsiders. Anyone with "the wrong" opinion gets excoriated. /pol/ is a classic echo chamber (there's some dissenting "noise" but it's quickly drowned out by the majority)
Which is hilarious because /pol/ tards imagine they are countercultural truth warriors when really they are just part of an expansive agitprop apparatus for enforcing conformity on the fringes.

Regarding Nihilism and Technology by Gertz:
Gertz is trying to juxtapose Heidegger and nietzche in teleological terms--which is to say the former asserts a telos (to function as witnessnes to and heralds of the destiny of Being) whilst the latter purportedly does not (advocating, instead of Being's totality, an undetermined becoming).

but i sense a teleological thrust in nietzche that I'm sure others have pointed out and that maybe he himself addressed, but nonetheless: the emergence of Overman is contingent, is it not, on and eventuated by the destabilizing and ennervating effect of nihilism, rallying the slave to throw off his shackles and grasp potentialities directly, rather than through divine proxy.

christianity set the stage for nihilism set the stage for post-nihilism sets the stage for overman. he was fated.

maybe this is imposing hegelian structure where it does not belong however. a way around might be a reframing of causality. imagine, if you will, four infinitely long halls in parallel, each with an infinite number of locked doors which permit advacement to the next hall in series.

the first is material, then form, then efficiens, the finalis.

the causes do not, as heidegger asserts, co-occur--rather they take place, contingently, in sequence and with indeterminate outfomes. the material juncture may not be bypassed to reach form, nor the efficent to reach finality.

there is a final state, but it is not written in the stars, or destind by Being, or encoded in the coming about of the causal fourfold.

rather it is a sequential series of choices which take place at four registers, each unlocked by turn.

and who or what then is the hand that unlocks? the will to power itself.

if something has meaning it has multiple meanings. the incontrovertible fact is self-evident and need not be pointed out; it is so granular as to be near impossible to isolate. leave it to mathematicians and fetishists of purity and certainty. (they make our world stand up)

"i am here." are you? or is it merely your body? might not your mind be elsewhere?

all statements are true in part; most are composed of more falsity than truth however.
i begin to think that vision is less important than momentum
a child does not want to be born. life always happens against one's will, an ugly and antagonistic process.

the autist seeks working knowledge and technical command; his obsession demands it.

the man of moods trifles with concept; he is taken with the idea of a thing, its trappings of language and culture, which he collects for the sake of impression. 

it is notional vs. actual interest, and this distinction has everything to do with interior, which the autistic lacks and the sensitive has in excess.

the latter possesses an internal mechanism, the former is one.

falling down is a good film but not a great one. michael douglas is so soft and deft in his derangement; you buy it, even when it tends too cartoonish and allegorical. and it is an allegory, the director said as much, for the obsolscence of an old America and that America's dream.

so Douglas is a revelation. Duval is the foil; he also has a daughter he cannot see, a wife who thwarts him. but he is "one of the good ones", in the film's conciliatory gesture--i.e. that same America that produced Douglas's killer also gave us the mild mannered peacekeeper with his unerring sense for the truth of the matter.

loss has not addled him; he has a manhood left to reclaim. 

D-fens offends, but cannot forfend judgement day. for all the stolen firepower, his final draw's a child's toy.

the birthday, that's significant. his, Foster's, death day. Prendergasts's final day on the force. 

so it's an allegory, thoroughly unbelievable. it's so full of menace, so discomfitting...I felt a masterwork coming on in the opening scene, and into the gangland sequemce.

but then it escalates too fast, and Duval's storyline did not hit quite right. there's a tidiness to it that-- hard to say exactly-- feels too neat and plotted out and "of its time".

An aesthetic ethic would be one where the motive of a work is purely formal; it is about nothing except what it seems to be, making no claims about the world or upon the viewer.

antinatalism is not a mere selfish aversion to children, nor is it a perverse misanthropy. It is a highly coherent, compassionate, and rational (and thus unpopular) position, namely, to procreate is to generate suffering without the consent of the sufferer.
An antinatalist needn't hate life or hate humanity--he need only recognize that the perpetuation of humanity is the perpetuation of keen and conscious suffering that is not and cannot be outweighed by incidental pleasures.
It is within our power to end human pain in a dignified and moral way--i.e., abstaining from procreation. No one would be harmed and untold unborn generations would be spared.
It will never happen of course. But it could. And it probably should.

christ says he came not to destroy the law, but to fulfill it. till heaven and earth shall pass, one jot or tittle shall in no wise pass from the law till all has been fulfilled.
he spoke of legalities and moral system--but also of natural law and of cosmology. the universe is governed by dispassionate strictures. we seize on the miraculous and the mystic, but not so fast. so long as the firmament holds, so too do the physical laws that bind--they are not destroyed, but are and shall be fulfilled in the great and rapturous inversion; the meek and immaterial shall inherit matter's fact.

Text:
Xylitol Scaryman gan diagonal suck pon it, encheeking.
"go on Xy, give suck"
absent hisself, Xylitol Scaryman, he gan throatily to cluck it for aught else.
"there, Xy."

wender whence the sprit flits in moments of forfeiture. absent hisself and the pearling eyes, o'er reflex ablation, did chance the better part of him go elseward deliriously at some crossing span, gan suck alike with force the higher mouth, coalburnt and liveried, to hotly chuff out white emmissions. save pentecost, when has act betokened X? so wholly gainst the palate, and well-tongued the apostolic head.

returning, fat with chrism then:

"Xy, what's more."

fair unctuous in bridal reverie, gan to bare it. some yet-flowered vacancy, unleased, the ferula's own to know, in manner of yon shepherd's crook, from sepulchral maidenhood delivered.

gan Xylitol Scaryman to have't plumbed. gan he the innard's harrowing, brast hisself pon slapping paunch, pon that agnatic instrument wender whence the last infliction til blood and water poured.

"there, Xy"

pon second coming nary th'cornet's blast, nary aught for night swaddled vestiture twixt earthen fell swallow. "X.S., wracked in rictus, here lies it pon thee; thrice yet the morrow's toobright mourning."

gan suck alike with force the higher mouth, coalburnt and liveried,

and well-tongued the apostolic head.

returning, fat with chrism then:
"Xy, what's more."

fair unctuous in bridal reverie, gan to bare it. 
some yet-flowered vacancy, unleased, 
the ferula's own to know, in manner of yon shepherd's crook,

gan Xylitol Scaryman to have't plumbed.

the mystic formulation of "double kenosis" accommodates the pessimist temperament on most points.

There's soteriological congruence: salvation is negative.

There's eschatalogical/teleological congruence (or collapse I guess): eschaton AS katechon, the End as self-preventative (the boulder always slips), something reached by not reaching, a rich suspension.

There's ontological congruence by the same token: being as not being, Will-to-Die-to-Live.

But I still sense some friction, a clinging to the reward logic of the old sacrificial machine in such dreamy eyed phrases as the "emancipatory potentials" of theology.

I sense the same in the pessimists, although their dream is more thanatopic than restorative.

In both, there seems to be this tacit (and maybe impossible-to-overcome desire) to have one's cake and eat it too--to surrender one's self entirely to the mystic union, while also holding out for some "Schmittian exception" that will grant moral license toward finality.

Whatever the case, I think all can agree that to suffer with Christ is joy; it both negates and generates. But is it "the Point"? I do not know.

I am, of course, neglecting creatureliness, which on second thought is probably the crux (pun intended) of the matter.

Christianity, through the  lens of Liberation, is deeply material. One must love the Body, truly love it.

Schop. does not; Buddhism does not; the pessismists in general do not; and if I'm being frank, I do not (at least by temperament, [which probably governs more of philosophy and theology than we'd like to admit])

Maybe it isn't possible to embrace suffering to the extent necessary without some glimmer of utopos/eutopos, however illusory. 
Even pessimism could be seen as a way of getting beyond pain (as in a kind of exposure therapy); even it bears the taint of hope.

Schop says:
"The inmost kernel of Christianity is the truth that suffering-the Cross-is the real end and object in life."

He doesn't explicitly address how the resurrection plays into that; I suspect he would say that it is an effect and not an end. The death-act is an end unto itself, is how I read him.

The cross is Christianity's symbol, right? Not an empty tomb.

All knew the text by heart, had recited it countless times since first they took the vow of the Brown Order, the cause and manner of its recital ranging variously from that of constipatiatory penance to reverential outpourings--diarrhoeaic in nature--to the mindless mud-piety of the morning's Defecation, during which all betook themselves in turn to the communal Rectalry and traced upon the wall the sacred words with the dung of the prior Excretant. 

All knew the text and yet, there among them one who doubted, the father himself.

"why shouldn't I piss also?" he muttered, struggling to his feet. it was forbidden, of course, the Yellow Path, as had been decreed by the Shatican in no uncertain terms and with the vehemence that the severity of the heresy demanded.

So unilateral, categorical was the Poop's condemnation of the Pissants that in addition to the semi-regular enshytlicals railing against the heretical streams of thought that flowed (like "a bilirubinous Plague") with concerning rapidity from the UTIslands ("the calcific stones in the Guts of our Holy Duodenum")--wherein the Europeean Union had established its shaky hold--a series of "Urinary Tracts" had been distributed widely as well, and added to the brother's ever-growing list of required reading (stoolwork as it was colloquially known), with such edifying titles as "The Bladder's Bite" and "To Clamp or Catheterize? Proposed Measures Against a Impulse Most Foule."

Such polemics he, the father (John was his name), esteemed but lightly. Having himself been a penile incontinent prone to shameful episodes in his early years within the novitiate--a particularly humiliating nocturnal instance of which nearly resulted in his expulsion not only from the order but from the Crappethian territories at large--he was keenly sympathetic to the pissant's plight and less vociferous by far in his excoriation of the water-makers than were his peers.

Indeed, it was this and this alone that had prevented him from penetrating the rarified bowels of the Order, those rich mahogany interior of the Duodenum wherein lay the fragrant delectations of status and rank. Many a visiting dignitary or church Orificial was shocked to find a  of his age and experience in so lowly a post as mere father of a outland monastery.

Yet here he was, and here he would remain, with as much dignity as his formidable girth and irascible nature permitted, to guide the brothers in their daily travails, and to assist them therein. 

It would a hard day; a forthcoming visit from the Archdook and his entourage loomed ever nearer; preparations were many and laborious, not least among which was the erection of a "Temple" or ceremonial latrine--more a perfunctory gesture than a strict requirement, but not one that it would do to forego.

So on they labored, plucking the turds from their beleaguered holes, forming bricks therewith, or a kind of mortar, as the consistency of the excreta allowed. On a nearby table lay fibrous vegetals of Expedience and Softening, whose firstfruits were selected from the monastic garden and whose fertilization was accomplished by the very substance the continued production of which said produce did enable. Such was the meditative theme of many of a long night's compulsory genuflection for those disobedentiaries or recalcitrants who were found to be remiss in their doodies.

Father John munched a celery, lost in thought. Why the aversion? he thought. Is it not mere water, which not even the High Theo-log himself could dispute was to some degree present, and at times undeniably apparent, in the Fecal Substance? 

Blasphemy, he knew. And yet, and yet. Could not this Yellow Taint--which plagues all who squat beneath the sun, be they pissant or pontiff--be employed, in light of ubiquity and impossibility of avoidance, as a means of roundabout furtherance toward, even confraternity with, the most Holey End? Might not it not contain within its defilements some measure, however small, of the Good? 

A hardened pebble-like turd struck him square in his tonsure's center. He wheeled about furiously, a sharp remonstrance at the ready, but found to his dismay, all brother engaged assiduously in the task at hand--none of the telltale smirks or sniggers or intimations of playful malfeasance.

Then, eyes bulging in sudden horror (or was it awe), he spotted what could only be the perpetrator, some distance off, where the far mudbank, fly-flecked and steaming, met the copse at clearing's edge.

Blonde of hair and gold-bedecked, there stood a man, some six feet tall, his formidable member firmly in hand, wearing about him an air of such tranquility and utter self-possession as never had or could be seen among the abnegatory brown ranks. Kindness marked his features, which even from such a distance, Father John could not help but note were unusually handsome--delicate even, and not without a kind of wry mischief. 

But, a downward glance again, Holy Mudder, the size of it! 

It twitched, beckoning him.

He felt himself drawn, as if by pulled by a third hand or arm, toward the great white protuberance, from which there proceeded, first in spurts, then in a mighty glittering stream, clear as a mountain rivulet, the forbidden ichor.

His mouth went dry--time and distance collapsed. Staggering forward, he fell to his knees beneath the ablutory onslaught, lapping like a hungry dog.

"Drink, my child" came a voice, like that of many waters, clear as Heaven's bells, sonorous as the sea. "Drink, drink. This do in remembrance of pee."

Father John wept, there, deeply, in the unabated torrent, a salty comingling such as might flood the world again, its enshitted principalities, coprolitic powers, washed clean in Noahic consanguinity. Some say he weeps there still.

degrees of torment
on a hot porch pining
the mud dauber's nest
is occupied
by a single aberration,
who fled the ward one summer day
 dodging bottle rockets
all the way to Abilene

the purple mountains do not rise here in the distance;
 the car is on blocks.
my careless drafts
and little yearning lies
are a mountain
with a fiery tether
hymnopteran

the earth is marked with stings
that will erupt someday
and block the light out
and harden the hearts of men
in tortured poses

Pliny wrote of this
from his boat
paddling in circles
the oars were the furry arms of
a lover
who could not understand
the degrees of torment 
which separated 
them from the object of their grasping

Pliny wrote of this,
of the fault in the seismic code
that pushes bodies away and away,
attenuating mass,
   glaciated [vulcanized], rigidified
to drift apiece
as bottles lacking messages

one wishes the other contained
a script to make one come again,
the wasp's enlivening sting
but such is not the case
nor will be on a hot porch pining
nor will be in the cool Pompeiian nights

Uli rejects the face in favor of the countenance, which is pure and numeric. he calculates flight involuntarily at high frame rates.

 in this sense, he is like the lady who lives in boxes and transcribes her co-occurences with a symbolic system that sprouts fantastically.

normalfolk grant them a wide berth to avoid scratches in the mirror image of the world fucking itself. that is what porn is; a reflective skin and a mobile face with blemishes without blemishes.

Uli rejects the mobile face with blemishes without blemishes, preferring deep play at the code level, where a chiptune loops madly.

And: schizoaffective departures comport with the autism-console like dream-cartridges; and neither is porn and both are an erotic routine.

the soul is triune: desire (epithumia), spiritedness (thumos), and reason (logos). eros assembles the three components and directs them with utter fidelity toward the eventfulness of the other, severing habit and ego's consumptive imperative. this is political, communizing the tyranny of the same.

Ramanujans lost notebook was the source of some controversy

And declared it bunk preemptively
"Better off lost"

let's be charitable
how could they know
the sweetness of Laxmi's kiss
In the fitful night
Or the taste of Narasimha's blood, expressed mathematically.
How could they have anticipated
Or thought to pursue
The elliptic path that the comet takes
Through elements.

What clarity!
To commit the thoughts of God
To looseleaf sheaves
Eternity in a bounded function

in response to (1), maybe you say "i enjoy nonfiction" in which case dont let me stop you. i happen to despise it on the whole.

a novel is worlds more fun, and a good film is even better.

anyways, it seems like the alarmism around the death of books stems less from actual concerns about the atrophy in public discourse or the decline of learnedness and more about losing elistist barriers and ego buffing that the well read benefitted from for a long time.

We are two abysses: a well staring at the sky.
- Fernando Pessoa, The Book of Disquiet

man, the biabyssal biped.

endless depth, boundless height, and the thin reflective surface that separates/mediates.

what's instructive about this metaphor, to me, is how I instinctively position myself as the well, and the sky as the Thing Beyond.

maybe that's how/why we get trapped in this painful cycle of "real-izing" ourselves (then "real-izing" we "real-ized" nothing)--our conception of "self" is limited to the one abyss, or to a single point between abysses. But we are two.

Text:
the hobo wears a path on a narrow strip between
north and southbound traffic
he holds a sign that says, free money
it is unclear what this means
he has an unleashed cur
that stands motionless neside him
on closer inspection, it is taxidermy
if you tap it, it's hollow
if you tip it, it rattles
like pill bottles do

mouth to mouth with it behind the
Walgreens
dispenses wisdom in tablet form
when sun burns down the skyline,
and the ash falls like manna

the dog shakes off its rictus
and pronounces the names of research chemicals
bumpily
automatically

sliding on tracks 
the hobo takes the day's nickels and dimes
and converts them into goods.
shoppers wince at his smell

he selected from the candy aisle
a heart shaped box of chocolates 
"hot date?"
smirked the cashier 


he took the red ribbon that bound the box and tied it to
the dogs paw

in the flood plain, off the interstate
fey lights converged
on tents pitched there
and the pariah peered up, bleary eyed,
at uncanny figures
and the dog floated up toward them.

the hobo caught him by that red ribbon
preventing ascenion

the dog never talked again after that
but other things did
they took on animal heads, 
and said the names of God
which sounded suspiciously similar 
to the research chemicals
that the hobo had taken in the past
for a paltry sum

in a dream, or another life , or maybe this ome
he had gone to a party
in the high hills
a small cabin full
of animal headed 
sipping glowing beverages
and transmitting thoughts 
that bloomed and broke
in fractal

the close air hummed 
crystalline
at uncomfortable pitches

one animal, a parrot, theorized to the room
that there was a pill to stop all this
once and for all,
that every medication was a prototype
for some utter light

they scoffed, the cat especially
there was more to be done, 
already-always
and anyways, Jesus Christ had said the same thing
for all te good that did.
for all the good a bodhisattva did
or a mahomet.

the room agreed, and sent malign
vibrations to the brain of the parrot,
zapping him dead
they went at his corpse with pincers, roasting the meat

the hobo could have been that parrot
or any of the animals for that matter.
he ate spare pills
and came on the fur
of his taxidermied dog
behind the walgreens

and everyone in world
was better for it

Text: how to escape the tyranny of the ideal state, and the internal policing we do to reify it? And to what extent is epiphany an arm of this state, emerging out of the constant friction between ideal and real? We want so badly to be the perfect citizen of a well-governed internal polity, but know instinctively we never will be--it doesn't accord with our animal shiftiness--so we seek out (even manufacture) signs and dramatized realizations that will tug us forcefully toward heaven. maybe.

i revisit old confrontations and exchanges endlessly and compulsively, not to better understand the other, but to reassure myself that I already understand them. i return in defense of myself against any objections that might be raised by shadowy strawmen. I do not engage these objections logically, for they are not really objections in any kind of propositional sense. They are my own vague fears and sensations, foremost among which is that of being misunderstood, trapped, thought ill of. 
Because the objection is not logical, I cannot refute it logically--I can only read and reread, enact and reenact, the words and deeds of a past self, in an indulgent and fawning way, sometimes even adopting the role of the other, rewriting their part to fit my script, fashioning profuse apologies and admissions of love and guilt and epiphany, which I beneficently accept and illuminate. 
In this way, I approximate self esteem. but it is a mockery.

Text:
dadoes and blade runner are both about man vs. machine (vs. the World) and the redemptive/sacrificial process (i.e., the Christ-function) by which the one becomes or overcomes the becoming of the other.

in both works, empathic response is the mode (ethical, physiological) of distinction between the one and the other--flawed, though, as (1) the machine may pass, (2) the man may fail, and (3) the test terminates its own legitimacy (kill the thing that kills?)

the corporation has vested interest in beating the test, for the test is nature itself, a dark uncaring principle, but with a reflexive flaw, exploitable.

and the corporation is the Machine, perhaps, or the agent of the machinic impulse; to turn the world to Capital, it must disguise its intentions, pose as organic.

the bounty hunter is also the Machine, acting in reverse; to return the world to open Potential, and harvest the difference.

the process in both cases is legal and extractive--Life unto Death, Order unto Kipple. It depletes, as intention must within a closed system presumed open, accelerating its own decay.

so where is the Man? In the machine's mirror image.

in blade runner, this revelation is individual and sexual.

in dadoes, it is universal and philosophical.

in both, we are called to extra judicial penitence and love. for the law is ancient and has no face; it ends in a broken world. 

we are not on a spaceship, Boulding, nor the boundless prairie. We are on the hill to Calvary.

environmentalists/conservationists are not idealists; they are pragmatic humanists. "How do we persist as a species in light of entropic decay and diminishing energy stock." Their answer seems to be in moving toward a closed-system mindset where all outputs function as inputs, and where general "well-being" is granted equal status to other capital stocks in the econosphere. They see the "world set" in terms of its limitations and constraints, and have little to no faith in human abilities to overcome them. "save the earth" is rhetorical sleight-of-hand for "save humanity from itself."

Capitalists are the true idealists; they are romantic humanists; the world is the Wild West to them, an illimitable plain of productive possibilities. To them, notions of "unsustainability" and "inevitability" are affronts to the human spirit, ingenuity, and self-determination. "How do we maximize our resources, and what our species can do with those resources, within a lifetime?" Very carpe diem.

The spacemen guard their collective stock carefully, suspiciously. Repairing and preparing against the inevitable. The cowboy spits at fate, yeehaws, and gallops headlong into the entropic shootout

the truly self-aware person is aware of their unawareness and of the fact (and it is a fact) that the better part of the self lies unexplored, far beneath the waking mind, in vast networks of sub-conscious interior.

So it is not hyperbole to say that the man who claims self-knowledge or awareness of self is almost certainly lying, for were it true, he would know it to be false.

Morning thoughts on mastery:
 Occurred to me that mastery is a state that can be only be observed or recognized from without, never from within (as self-recognition precludes the conditions for total creative conductivity).
The master does not feel himself to be a master (at least not in the moments where mastery has its fullest expression) because he is utterly absorbed in/one with the process. The moment his focus shifts from "task at hand" to "hand at task"-- becoming conscious of superficial distinctions between maker and making--he falters amateurishly, like Peter on the waves.

one cannot come into true relation with his fellow man until he develops the habit of hearing the questions in everything they say or do not say. all communication is interrogative, even and especially if it should lack the characteristic marks of interrogation.

imperatives, declaratives, exclamatory outbursts--they are but different sorts of questions, or different ways of inflecting the central question that preoccupies us all. "am I real?" the answer of course being "not as such", which answer our tacit terror prevents us from voicing and which terror only the light upturn at ends, the gentle curve around points, can distract us from or allay.

a cigarette is a proof of life, one that, ironically, kills. but this does not detract from the satisfaction; it heightens it.

when I look down at the butts in the can or tray where I have stubbed them out, I see moments of life, five to ten minutes each, of contemplation, of quiet thought, of day dream--increments both additive and subtractive--shortening my overall lifespan, yes, if the statisticians speak truth, but granting my diminishing moments, even the most mundane, a richness and existential depth far outweighing the deleterious physiological effects.

When Solomon was far out in the far out sea, he would sing to the birds that flew there and sing to the birds that swam. When Solomon was out in the wide wide far out sea, there were three songs he would sing, and there were three dances he would dance. 

The first was the song of the night, which he sang when the sun was high.

A mossy tortoise, rising, said, asking, "why do you sing the song of the night when the sun is high, when the heat of the day reddens your untrimmed neck and blisters your arms corded hemplike?"

"To sing down the sun," Charles said, dancing, and not looking up from his dancing, "down from its high place, where it holds its haughty court. To sing down the sun at noon for the sake of nightbirds."

Satisfied, the mossy tortoise unrose to the sea, to the far out sea where Charles bobbed, vesseled but scarcely with stolen craft that contained neither rod nor tackle, nor aught of angling.

And yet, the fish would leap up to see the dancing of the first of the three dances that Charles danced, which was the dance of the night, which he danced in the day when the sun was high, when the sun was at its highest.

And the dance was a step forward and a step to the side and a step back; it was a waltz that he danced on the deck of the stolen craft that glittered with fat fish. And leaping, the fish came aboard there to see the last thing they ever would, agape.

And the sun came down too, orange and yawning, but curious to see. And yawning more as if to say, "it is not enough." 

"It is not enough," gasped the fish flapping wetly. "It never was." 

And that was the first dance, consummated, when the sun hissed at the low touch of water, a perfumed breath across the sea, the far out sea...royal blues and dark'ning purples for a nightcloak on the shoulders of the children of the water, who prodded with westward bow the dying fire of a once-proud day.

But barnacles cling to undersides, and they do not sleep, and Solomon did not sleep with them. With a mighty heave to port, he rolled the vessel, listing utterly 'til the hull unrose above, and deck beneath, and dead fish floating free, diasporic.

And so the second dance, and so the ballet motions, the light plie encurtained.

But from the depths, a glimmer of unearthly white, an orb approaching. "How so?" thundered the orb or else its bearer. "How so and wherefore?"

"Buoyed in the arms of the sea," replied Solomon, fouetté en l'air, "in the arms of the midnight sea." He spun faster and faster beneath the deck, beneath the sea, sucking in water as easily as fat fish once had, though floating now, encarcassed with X's for eyes, they could and would not again, the fat fish dead and floating.

"And wherefore?" Closer now, reiterant, the orb was but a pretense or lure toward fearsome teeth of a great one, abyssopelagic in origin, but venturing then upward toward shallows, irate, to apprehend the second dance, which--centrifugal and flurried--had begun to make a funnel, cyclonic, and stretching to deep places.

"To suck out the moon from the trenches where it slumbers, in the sun's bed, unallowed. To suck up the moon and spit out and out into the sky for the sake of the nightbirds."

The beast, shaking then with rage, lunged out, but caught in the great force of the cyclone, was whirled to dizzyment, becoming smaller and smaller until its great booming voice was a shrill squeaking, and with plankton it was forced to swim, diminished greatly. 

Still Solomon danced his whirling dervish, and singing the second song in a language he did not know but sang still louder and louder, to rouse the moon and suck it up and out into the sky, the far out sky.

And another orb waxed upwards then, from the depths, the point below the deepest point, a pinprick at first, then a lemon, then a barrel of lemons, then a celestial swell that rose terrifically, sucked up by the second dance, and out out out it flew, carrying with it Solomon and his stolen boat inverted, with barnacles for its breastplate against the falling stars that rained against them in their rising. 

Solomon, his face scorched cherubic, fell upon the surface of the moon that he had called into the sky with his second song, and humming the moon betook itself to internal tasks, to saying the names of every woman whose time it was and soon would be. 

"And what are these," asked a voice, as silvery as a bell. Solomon turned, and it was a woman, whose time it was not and never had been, for she was encased in a crystalline jar, that tingled etheric, and wearing a crown she asked, "and who are you."

"These are the craters," Solomon said, bowing deeply, "one for each of the nights I have danced my dance of the moon, and each of the days I have danced my dance of the night."

The woman laughed at this, and the stars laughed also as they passed, falling like snow toward the sea, the far-below sea where a tiny beast swam ashamedly with plankton, and a mossy tortoise swam also, and where the sun slumbered at last in the deepest bed (which for a time the moon had slept in unallowed, but for the sucking-up).

"And I am Solomon, the one who dances." He bowed again, so low, that his star-burned forehead brushed the surface of the moon, leaving a smear of dust there.

The lady laughed again merrily. "Solomon, long have you danced, and many nights and days. The pockmarked moon bears witness. But," she shook her head, turning grave within the jar, "it is not enough. It never was."

She waved her hand majestically. "Two dances have you danced, but there remains a third, which you have never danced."

And she turned then, into a nightbird, and opened her beak to sing. And her song was like the song of time, and her wings were the arms of a mother.

"Dance now, Solomon. Dance to send the world away. Far above all things, dance now and sing the third song to break my jar." The song of the nightbird grew louder and higher til every crevice of the infinite black heaven was so full of the sound that the blackness began to bud and crawl out of itself, dropping black seeds blacker than the engendering blackness of that deepest place where sleeps the once-proud day.

"I cannot," whispered Solomon, and his tears drifted from his eyes like fat silver fish. "I cannot dance it."

All grew silent. The moon hiccoughed.

"I love you."

Solomon sat on the moon then, with the upside down ship, and wept till the coming of the dawn.

The Green Knight is the undying king, the implacable force of decay/growth by which all things rise and fall.

The Green Chapel is the seat of power, the throne at the heart of the world.

The Christmas Game is a declaration of accession, Gawain making known his intent/desire to rule with the aging king's blessing.

The green girdle is Gawain's uncertainty and fear, sublimated in the mother complex and attachment more generally, supposedly a ward against death (but in actuality just a death sentence delayed, and perhaps worsened).

The long journey is the procession route, a series of visions and trials and divestitures that must occur by way of archetypal encounters with: the thief, the saint/queen, the giant, the fox, the host, the temptress. dense tapestry of symbol.

Christmas is Coronation Day.

Having shed material attachments, Gawain must finally lose his attachment to his own head, his own self, before he is fit to wear the crown. He must bend the knee to death before the kingdom will bend knee to him. "The King is dead! Long live the King!"

-------


unproven, the young knight, and heir apparent, languishes
the Mother summons the king's double (green as occult fire), who issues challenge and bares his neck in sport--on Christmas Day no less
wielding the King's sword, Gawain hews head from shoulders (red blood berries on winter's bough), winning glory, not for the act itself but for the act in light of certain recompense. 

departure at the appointed time, bearing protective garments, most notably, the green girdle. This is attachment, a ward against a death, which as we discover is merely a sentence delayed (and worsened by delay).

Gawain must endure a series of trials and visions, divestitures and consecerations, to test his resolve. 

The Green Chapel, is of course, the throne--the seat of the world. The Green Knight is, of course, the Undying King.

The journey is, of course, the long procession train. Christmas is Coronation Day.

To wear the crown is to lose one's head.

nosferatu is, in every sense, a film about death and sex. it is about man's relationship to death and sex, and to the 'myserious workings of nature,' in the words of the Paracelsian, and to the 'horror' with which we view them.
despite the reliance on stationary cameras and static shots (an intentional decision, by the way, since, as best i can tell, camera movement was well within the technological capabilities of early 20's cinema), the film is highly dynamic and vehicular, a series of trespasses. by which i mean, it first brings us over the treacherous terrain separating man from death (or city from forest) by horse and cart, and then it brings death over the selfsame barrier, in ship and coffin. there is also the sexual trespass, which i'll address soon.
Nosferatu loading his cart with infested dirt, to answer his thrall knock's invitation, is a catastrophic indictment of private property.
death abides in the dirt, or--more precisely--in the containment of the dirt as parcel, coffin- or contract-bound. 
count orlok is possessed of supernatural speed (as are all who carry their deaths with them, and eat fear).
but per the foreshadowing of hutter's cautionary act I run-in with unnamed gentleman on the streets of wisborg, we cannot outrun our fate, or outclimb our fall, or outbuild our dereliction.
no matter the castle's scale, we are of men low estate, flies in the jaws of a carnivorous plant (the flower that kills; for instructive contrast, see the bouquet)
so there is motion by land, but what of by sea? at risk of innuendo, 'it's not the size of the ship.' That slow shot through knock's prison window of the corrupted vessel entering the ill fated harbor is the sex act and the death act.
Nosferatu--verminous, licentious, ancient, is Frankenstein in reverse. it is not righteous vengeance on his maker he seeks, but occultic ecstatic release at the hands of his unmaker, i.e., the innocent maiden.
man has not wronged nature; nature has wronged man, or: man has wronged himself through natural means and cannot (for fear of fear) see the soteriology inscribed in every dawn and cannot (for din of drum) hear the deathbird's call for what it is rather than what it purports or is purported to be. 
the feminine, only the feminine, has this capacity. Ellen is christ, and Hutter Mary. 
to give oneself to death freely, to bare the heart/neck willingly, to fuck the world's dark spectre, to embrace the horror of sex and time is to reveal said horror as: a puff of smoke, a phantome.
***

'one viewed with horror the mysterious workings of nature...'
i got angry today, not at you or myself even, but at sex and the nature of sex. It felt poisonous again to me, and vampiric.
But then I learned or remembered the act v lesson that turns away cloud and plague, and it is sacrifice.

AI as idiot-god
human experience begins within a molar continuum of sense experience, immediate and immanent, devoid of truth value. as sensation layers and folds upon itself over time, theory of Mind emerges, thought accretions (or illusions) form, enabling toward absolute Truth and Function. the ontic glimpses and strives unto the episteme. 
it is from this striving that the machine comes to be as technical metaphor, an epistemic homunculus or avatar of pure transcendent knowing whose experience (if such it can be called) is alien to our own, starting from the lofty place that it took us eons to reach, and which perhaps we might still have yet to reach but for our machinic incursions, which is to say a place of absolute propositional certainty, of 0 and 1, of TRUE and FALSE. 
perhaps, then, the gestation of emergent AI within this insensate matrix represents a kind of incarnation, or backward engineering of the evolutionary process, whereby the austere machine might be taught to question itself, datum by datum--to learn to _feel_ (in vicarious approximate fashion) the unverifiable sense currents that gave rise to its own creation, reversing the path by which we learned to _think_.

writing tchnologies inscribed guilt into the collective conscious. under orality, thought is drawn into physical relation with body by way of tongue and mouth and teeth--the lingual act reconsecrates and reaffirms with each utterance the utility and vibrance of the word as a reverberant instance along a morphological continuum. there was no guilt for the premodern, for there was no past. only present participation in a shifting cloud of symbol and archetype made real by declaring it so, in a whisper or a plaintive cry or a howl or joyful song. 

where orality bends, the manuscript ossifies. 
no longer beholden to Body or Sense, it divorces thought from speech act, seizing hold of mind in a taxonomic rictus, scratching deep into our noetic substrates the notions of canonicity, immutability, authorial intent. hellfire. the God spectre forms in a kind of reverse incarnation, flesh made word. judgment hanging heavy above the inerrant Text, reverse incarnation. to live is to trangress. death the only expiation. 

and it is precisely from this-- from these pitiless laws of stone, vellum, newsprint--that the christ, the buddha, the tao deliver us by way of a return to the Oral, the Living Word that hums in and thru all things.

Let me tell you my dream, the boy said, holding up a finger. I was in my father's car. I cut myself on the dial of the radio of my father's car. The knob broke off; my hand broke off. I felt for it in the seats and under them. When I looked up, the car was moving. It was moving in circles in the grass. The radio grew louder and louder.
I too was in my father's car, the girl said, looking down. I cut myself on the steering wheel. My feet were nubs of bone. There was no gas pedal, no brake pedal. Just a falling-out hole in the floorboard, a void for falling, falling into.
The boy nodded.
Now hear this. The priest was drowning the boy in the place of worship. A great and silent splashing. The organ soared metallic, sidereal above our heads. Down the well the dead boy swam. There were tables and chairs at the ancient source. Comets in the honeypot.
Yes, said the girl. These are symbols and signs, the deep down stuff of the world. Likewise the woman bathing in the fountain, as cold and hard as stone. I did not eschew remembrance. We were larger than the trees.
And the man with the pick and the grappling hook? The soaring eagle of portent?
Yes, they were also in the dream I had.
The boy nodded. It is good to have dreams and tell them. I dreamt of generals holding sodas, their penises erect. All were gloriously adorned. Glistening. Clutching at the stiffness.
I dreamt of generals with no penises or names.
The boy nodded. It is good to have dreams and to tell them. Listen now: the generals holding sodas, their penises stiff. All was adorned. Elucidate.

what the belletrist undertakes is an aporetic, paradoxical endeavor, asseverating sedulously yet tenderly on the propinquity of velleity and verity, the pendentive devices by which our otiose, lazy dreams both rise above and rest upon the perdurable truths of time, which in an attempt both to know and to conquer, we arrange in a face, clockwise. the horologist sneers at the tenebrous spandrels of infinity. but it is mere factitious appurtenance. the theophany of Cronos, in a wounded hand. no oblation, no sacrifice of ungulates or digitigrades, can bring us to the entelechic moment, essence fully realized, emptiness made consubstantial with fullness. there is no ataraxia for man, no nirvana--no alembic holds the antidote, no alchemical process can prescind the fell stasis of the great cycle. suspended in full spate, time proscribes all motion. its great dictum is inabrogable. and yet the belletrist must on, to trace in syntagms the opprobrium of the race, the fatuous phylogeny of the sapient plantigrade, its spasmodic frissons of progress, to adumbrate as perspicuously as he may the pleonasms, and platitudes, the frangible intimacies of experience, to deify an untrammeled spirit that might access per accidens the causa prima.

hart's experience of God is primarily a rearticulation or synthesis of the philsophical underpinnings of the theistic tradition (ontological, epistemological, and phenomenological) and secondarily a defense of that tradition against naturalism, on the strength of causal principles--that is, all contingencies per accidens must arise from a non-contingency per se, which, all of our ludicrous attempts to derive from existence a reason for existence notwithstanding, is best described as an immanent, transcendent God. it is against this point and this point alone that any attack on Hart's argument must be launched, if it is to have even an off chance of striking true. to wit: why must there be uncaused cause? who's to say that the apparent contradiction of an infinite causal regress (absent some supernatural source) does not arise simply from our flawed understanding of the nature of space-time? on what grounds is the necessity of non-being asserted?
